stemHtml: During an automated en-route flight, the autotrim fails. What are the implications of such failure?

options.a: The autopilot will automatically switch to the backup autotrim.
options.b: The autopilot should disconnect, with an associated warning for the situation. The pilot flying must be ready to take controls.
options.c: The pilot must disconnect the autopilot and take the control of the aircraft in order to avoid a runaway autotrim condition.
options.d: The pilot flying should pay close attention to the CG, and be prepared for the aircraft to be out of trim in case the autopilot is disengaged.
